docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Decision Intelligence Most Organizations Don't Have a Data Problem. They Have a Decision Problem.

Data → Dashboards → KPIs → ML Predictions. Yet the final decision is still made using intuition, politics, and proxy metrics.

I help organizations redesign decision pipelines so that models, analytics, and strategy align with what actually matters.

How I Work Read the Articles → Core Belief Fitting data is not the same as understanding the world. The Architecture Data → Structural Model → Optimal Decision. The Goal Decisions that survive reality — not just dashboards that describe the past. The Broken Pipeline Enterprises built dashboards. They built ML models. They never built decisions.

For years, organizations invested in data platforms, dashboards, and machine learning to close the gap between insight and action.

The gap remains.

The actual decision pipeline still looks like this:

Data → Dashboard → Gut Feeling → Decision

Or at best:

Data → ML Prediction → KPI Optimization → Decision The Result
  • Precise but fragile
  • Predictive but not causal
  • Optimized for proxy metrics
  • Blind to uncertainty

The pipeline works until the world changes. And decisions change the world.

Core Concepts The mental models of Decision Intelligence. 📉 The Flaw of Averages

Decision-makers optimize for the "most likely" scenario. But E[f(x)] ≠ f(E[x]). Averages systematically break nonlinear systems. Point predictions are lies about certainty.

Explore the flaw → 🥗 Causal Salads

Organizations optimize metrics whose relationship to real utility is poorly understood. Disconnected KPIs and proxy objectives create fragile, misleading decisions.

Identify the salad → 🌍 Distribution Shift

A decision changes the world. Therefore, the prediction problem after intervention differs from the problem before. Black-box ML fails when the data-generating process shifts.

Understand the shift → The Maturity Model The Six Stages of Decision-Making.

Most organizations are stuck between Stage 3 and Stage 4. They have data, dashboards, and ML models, but they lack structural understanding and counterfactual reasoning.

My work focuses on helping organizations evolve to Stage 5: Structural Decision Intelligence, where causal structure, uncertainty, utility, and interventions are explicitly modeled.

Read the full framework → Stage 0-2 Intuition & Anecdotes Decisions from authority, instinct, and expert heuristics. Stage 3-4 (Where most are stuck) Dashboards & ML KPIs Data-driven gut feeling and point predictions optimized for proxy metrics. Stage 5 (The Goal) Structural Decision Intelligence Causal models, probabilistic forecasts, and counterfactual optimization. Who This Is For Bridging the gap between Data Science and the Boardroom. For Decision Makers

CEOs, executives, and business leaders who want robust planning under uncertainty, utility-aligned modeling, and a deeper understanding of what analytics can and cannot do.

  • Evaluate ML projects properly
  • Improve decision pipelines
  • Avoid analytical traps
Explore consulting → For Data Scientists

Practitioners who want to move beyond curve fitting, leaderboard optimization, and black-box prediction toward causal and structural reasoning.

  • Build decision-ready models
  • Master causal inference
  • Quantify uncertainty
Browse articles → Intelligence is not knowing more. It is deciding better.

If this resonates, we should talk.

Start a conversation Learn more about me →

docs.title: About
docs.text:
I am Ali Hosseinpouran — a Decision Intelligence Scientist working at the intersection of: - probabilistic modeling, - economics, - causal inference, - machine learning, - optimization, - and organizational decision-making.

My work focuses on designing systems that transform data into decisions that remain robust when the world changes.

Background: - Pure mathematics (probability & statistics) - Economics - Financial mathematics research - Bayesian ML and forecasting - Reinforcement learning and optimization - Executive education and consulting
